agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Parcel delivery logistics platform managing shipment lifecycle from creation through transit to delivery, with weight-based pricing tiers. Core workflow: create shipment with addresses, transition through states, charge based on weight bracket.","bounded_context":"Shipment Management — owns shipment lifecycle (creation, state transitions, delivery finality) and weight-based classification for pricing.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Shipment","definition":"A domain entity representing a parcel moving from a pickup address to a delivery address, with a defined weight and lifecycle state."},{"term":"Pickup Address","definition":"The origin location where the parcel is collected. Required at shipment creation."},{"term":"Delivery Address","definition":"The destination location where the parcel is delivered. Required at shipment creation."},{"term":"Shipment State","definition":"The current lifecycle phase of a shipment. Valid states: Pending, InTransit, Delivered."},{"term":"Pending","definition":"Initial state of a newly created shipment, before it has been picked up for transit."},{"term":"InTransit","definition":"State indicating the shipment is actively being transported. Irreversible — cannot return to Pending."},{"term":"Delivered","definition":"Terminal state indicating the shipment has reached its delivery address. No further state changes permitted."},{"term":"Weight","definition":"The physical mass of the parcel in kilograms. Must be positive and at most 1000 kg."},{"term":"Weight Bracket","definition":"A classification tier derived from shipment weight that determines pricing: Standard (0–10 kg], Heavy (10–50 kg], Freight (>50 kg)."},{"term":"Standard","definition":"Weight bracket for shipments weighing more than 0 kg up to and including 10 kg."},{"term":"Heavy","definition":"Weight bracket for shipments weighing more than 10 kg up to and including 50 kg."},{"term":"Freight","definition":"Weight bracket for shipments weighing more than 50 kg up to and including 1000 kg."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"A shipment requires both a pickup address and a delivery address at creation time.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"A shipment without both endpoints is physically meaningless — there is no parcel movement without an origin and destination. This is a data integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to create a shipment with a null or empty pickup address or delivery address must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"A shipment begins in the Pending state upon creation.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"State integrity — every shipment must enter the lifecycle at a defined starting point. Skipping Pending would corrupt the state machine.","testable_assertion":"A newly created shipment must have state = Pending."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"Once a shipment is InTransit, it cannot transition back to Pending.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"State machine integrity — reverse transitions would corrupt tracking, billing, and audit trails. Physically, a parcel that has left pickup cannot be 'un-picked-up'.","testable_assertion":"A state transition from InTransit to Pending must be rejected by the system."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Delivered is a terminal state — no further state transitions are permitted.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Delivery finality is a core invariant. Reopening a delivered shipment would break invoicing, proof-of-delivery records, and downstream integrations.","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to change the state of a Delivered shipment (to Pending, InTransit, or any other state) must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Shipment weight must be strictly greater than zero.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"A parcel with zero or negative weight is physically impossible and would break weight-based pricing calculations.","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to create or update a shipment with weight <= 0 must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Shipment weight must not exceed 1000 kg.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is a business policy limit, not a physical law. The company could raise this cap for oversized freight partnerships or special contracts.","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to create or update a shipment with weight > 1000 kg must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"Weight bracket Standard applies to shipments with weight > 0 kg and <= 10 kg.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Bracket boundaries are pricing policy — a VP could adjust the threshold from 10 kg to 15 kg without corrupting system state.","testable_assertion":"A shipment with weight 10 kg must be classified as Standard. A shipment with weight 10.01 kg must not be Standard."},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"Weight bracket Heavy applies to shipments with weight > 10 kg and <= 50 kg.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Pricing policy threshold, adjustable by business decision.","testable_assertion":"A shipment with weight 50 kg must be classified as Heavy. A shipment with weight 50.01 kg must not be Heavy."},{"id":"BR-9","rule":"Weight bracket Freight applies to shipments with weight > 50 kg and <= 1000 kg.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Pricing policy threshold, adjustable by business decision.","testable_assertion":"A shipment with weight 51 kg must be classified as Freight. A shipment with weight 1000 kg must be classified as Freight."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A user provides a valid pickup address, delivery address, and positive weight <= 1000 kg","when":"The user creates a shipment","then":"The shipment is created in Pending state with the correct weight bracket assigned."},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A user attempts to create a shipment without a pickup address","when":"The creation request is submitted","then":"The system rejects the request with a validation error indicating pickup address is required."},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A user attempts to create a shipment without a delivery address","when":"The creation request is submitted","then":"The system rejects the request with a validation error indicating delivery address is required."},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A shipment exists in Pending state","when":"The shipment is transitioned to InTransit","then":"The shipment state changes to InTransit successfully."},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A shipment exists in InTransit state","when":"An attempt is made to transition it back to Pending","then":"The system rejects the transition and the shipment remains InTransit."},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A shipment exists in InTransit state","when":"The shipment is transitioned to Delivered","then":"The shipment state changes to Delivered successfully."},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A shipment exists in Delivered state","when":"Any state transition is attempted","then":"The system rejects the transition and the shipment remains Delivered."},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A user provides a weight of 0 kg or negative","when":"A shipment creation is attempted","then":"The system rejects the request with a validation error indicating weight must be positive."},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A user provides a weight exceeding 1000 kg","when":"A shipment creation is attempted","then":"The system rejects the request with a validation error indicating weight exceeds the maximum."},{"id":"AC-10","given":"A shipment is created with weight 5 kg","when":"The weight bracket is calculated","then":"The shipment is classified as Standard."},{"id":"AC-11","given":"A shipment is created with weight 25 kg","when":"The weight bracket is calculated","then":"The shipment is classified as Heavy."},{"id":"AC-12","given":"A shipment is created with weight 75 kg","when":"The weight bracket is calculated","then":"The shipment is classified as Freight."}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Weight exactly at bracket boundary (10 kg)","expected_behavior":"Classified as Standard. The spec says '0-10 kg is standard', so 10 kg is the upper bound of Standard, not the lower bound of Heavy."},{"scenario":"Weight exactly at bracket boundary (50 kg)","expected_behavior":"Classified as Heavy. The spec says '10-50 kg is heavy', so 50 kg is the upper bound of Heavy, not the lower bound of Freight."},{"scenario":"Weight exactly at maximum (1000 kg)","expected_behavior":"Accepted and classified as Freight."},{"scenario":"Weight just above zero (e.g., 0.001 kg)","expected_behavior":"Accepted — weight is positive. Whether such a small parcel is operationally meaningful is a separate business question."},{"scenario":"Attempting Pending → Delivered transition directly (skipping InTransit)","expected_behavior":"Spec does not explicitly address this. Ambiguity — see escalation. Recommend requiring Pending → InTransit → Delivered as the only valid forward path."},{"scenario":"Shipment with both addresses identical (pickup = delivery)","expected_behavior":"Spec does not prohibit this. Likely a data quality issue but not explicitly a domain rule. System should accept unless additional validation rules are defined."},{"scenario":"Changing weight after shipment is InTransit or Delivered","expected_behavior":"Spec does not address weight mutability post-creation. If weight drives pricing and invoicing, changing it after transit begins is dangerous. Recommend immutability once InTransit."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Weight bracket classification directly determines pricing. Incorrect bracket assignment means over- or under-charging customers. Boundary errors at 10 kg and 50 kg thresholds are highest financial risk.","operational":"State machine integrity ensures reliable shipment tracking. Violations (e.g., Delivered shipments reopening) would corrupt dispatch queues, audit logs, and partner SLAs.","user":"Clear shipment lifecycle gives customers confidence in tracking. Rejected invalid transitions prevent confusing UI states. Weight validation at creation prevents downstream correction costs.","risk":"Primary risk is boundary ambiguity at weight thresholds — whether 10 kg is Standard or Heavy is a revenue-impacting decision. Secondary risk is the unspecified Pending → Delivered skip path which could be exploited to bypass operational checkpoints.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Operations / Dispatch","impact":"Rely on state machine integrity for routing and scheduling. Reverse transitions or skipped states would break dispatch workflows."},{"group":"Finance / Billing","impact":"Weight brackets drive invoicing. Misclassification at boundaries directly affects revenue accuracy."},{"group":"Customers (Shippers)","impact":"Expect consistent pricing based on weight and reliable shipment tracking. Violated state rules erode trust."},{"group":"Delivery Couriers","impact":"Depend on accurate state to know what to pick up and deliver. State corruption creates failed pickups and wasted routes."}]},"confidence":{"level":72,"reasoning":"The spec is clear on core state transitions and weight constraints but leaves several operationally important details unspecified: whether Pending → Delivered is a valid skip, whether weight is mutable post-creation, whether addresses can change after creation, and whether boundary values (10 kg, 50 kg) are inclusive upper bounds of the lower bracket or inclusive lower bounds of the upper bracket. I have made reasonable domain assumptions but these should be confirmed.","high_confidence_areas":["Shipment requires both addresses at creation","Delivered is terminal — no state changes permitted","InTransit cannot revert to Pending","Weight must be positive and <= 1000 kg","Three weight brackets exist: Standard, Heavy, Freight"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact boundary inclusivity at 10 kg and 50 kg thresholds","Whether Pending → Delivered is a valid transition","Whether weight or addresses are mutable after creation","Whether additional states (e.g., Cancelled, Failed) exist"],"assumptions":["10 kg boundary is inclusive to Standard (i.e., Standard is (0, 10] not [0, 10))","50 kg boundary is inclusive to Heavy","Only valid forward path is Pending → InTransit → Delivered","Weight is set at creation and immutable thereafter","No Cancelled or Failed states exist in the current model"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The spec says '0-10 kg is standard' and '10-50 kg is heavy'. The value 10 kg sits at both boundaries. Is 10 kg Standard or Heavy? This directly impacts pricing.","affected_stakeholders":["Finance / Billing","Customers (Shippers)"],"options":["10 kg is inclusive to Standard (upper bound)","10 kg is inclusive to Heavy (lower bound)"],"recommendation":"Treat 10 kg as Standard (upper bound inclusive) — industry convention is to keep the smaller/cheaper bracket inclusive at the boundary. Same logic: 50 kg is Heavy, not Freight."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Can a shipment transition directly from Pending to Delivered, skipping InTransit? The spec forbids InTransit → Pending and locks Delivered, but does not explicitly define the full set of valid transitions.","affected_stakeholders":["Operations / Dispatch","Delivery Couriers"],"options":["Allow Pending → Delivered (supports edge cases like in-store pickup)","Require Pending → InTransit → Delivered only (strict lifecycle)"],"recommendation":"Require strict sequential transitions (Pending → InTransit → Delivered). Skipping InTransit bypasses operational checkpoints and audit trail integrity."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The spec does not address whether weight or addresses can be modified after shipment creation, or after state transitions.","affected_stakeholders":["Finance / Billing","Operations / Dispatch"],"options":["Allow weight/address edits only in Pending state","Lock all fields after creation","Allow edits until InTransit"],"recommendation":"Allow modifications only while in Pending state. Once InTransit, weight and addresses should be immutable to protect billing accuracy and dispatch integrity."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}
agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Parcel delivery logistics platform managing shipment lifecycle from creation through transit to delivery, with weight-based pricing tiers. Core workflow: create shipment with addresses, transition through states, charge based on weight bracket.","bounded_context":"Shipment Management — owns shipment lifecycle (creation, state transitions, delivery finality) and weight-based classification for pricing.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Shipment","definition"
